structure.description: Catalog body renders as month-grid (or week-grid) keyed by date. Existing dates show as filled cells with link to the note; empty dates show 'create' affordance that instantiates from configured template. Switches the body archetype from list to calendar when the entity has a date primary key + template field.

rationale.hypothesis: Time-keyed entities with templates (journals, standups, retros) have a discoverability problem in flat lists: 'did I write Tuesday?' is a worse question than 'show me the calendar.' Calendar grid makes existence and gaps visible at a glance; template makes creation a one-click action with consistent structure.

evidence.description: Calendar plugin: month grid, dotted/filled cells, click to open or create from Daily Note template
Built-in journals view as date-keyed feed/grid
Database-with-Calendar-view + page templates achieve the same effect

counterexample.description: Issues are date-touched but not date-keyed; calendar grid would force a non-natural axis
Messages are time-stamped but creation is event-driven, not template-driven

shouldMatch.reason: MoodEntry has date primary key + recurring structure — calendar grid fits
HabitLog is date-keyed; calendar with checkmarks per habit fits

shouldNotMatch.reason: Listings are not date-keyed — calendar imposes wrong axis
Transactions are timestamped but not template-driven; flat list with date grouping is better
